{"replace_all": false, "file_path": "/Users/apple/Documents/projects/eagle-point_new/Practicum-Assessment/repo/tests/unit/test_rbac_service.py", "old_string": "        assert rbac_service.can_access_cohort(user, seeded_assessment["cohort_id"]) is True
", "new_string": "        assert rbac_service.can_access_cohort(user, seeded_assessment["cohort_id"]) is True


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
#  resolve_scope tests
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------


def test_resolve_scope_global_returns_all(app, seeded_assessment):
    """scope:global resolves to every active cohort."""
    with app.app_context():
        all_cohorts = Cohort.query.filter(Cohort.is_active.is_(True)).all()
        user = User.query.filter_by(username="advisor1").first()
        result = rbac_service.resolve_scope(user, {"scope:global"})
        assert result == {c.id for c in all_cohorts}


def test_resolve_scope_self_returns_member_cohorts(app, seeded_assessment):
    """scope:self resolves to only the cohorts where the user is a member."""
    with app.app_context():
        student = User.query.filter_by(username="student1").first()
        result = rbac_service.resolve_scope(student, {"scope:self"})
        assert result == {seeded_assessment["cohort_id"]}


def test_resolve_scope_cohort_returns_specific(app, seeded_assessment):
    """scope:cohort:<id> resolves to just that cohort."""
    with app.app_context():
        user = User.query.filter_by(username="advisor1").first()
        cid = seeded_assessment["cohort_id"]
        result = rbac_service.resolve_scope(user, {f"scope:cohort:{cid}"})
        assert result == {cid}


def test_resolve_scope_dept_includes_subdepartments(app, seeded_assessment):
    """scope:dept resolves to all cohorts under the user's department tree."""
    with app.app_context():
        # Create a second sub-department under the same department with its own cohort
        cohort = db.session.get(Cohort, seeded_assessment["cohort_id"])
        klass = db.session.get(Class, cohort.class_id)
        major = db.session.get(Major, klass.major_id)
        subdept = db.session.get(SubDepartment, major.sub_department_id)
        dept = db.session.get(Department, subdept.department_id)

        sub2 = SubDepartment(department_id=dept.id, name="Sub2", code="S2")
        db.session.add(sub2)
        db.session.flush()
        school = db.session.get(School, major.school_id)
        major2 = Major(name="Major2", code="M2", school_id=school.id, sub_department_id=sub2.id)
        db.session.add(major2)
        db.session.flush()
        class2 = Class(name="Class2", year=2026, major_id=major2.id)
        db.session.add(class2)
        db.session.flush()
        cohort_new = Cohort(name="Cohort Dept2", class_id=class2.id, internship_term="2026 Spring", is_active=True)
        db.session.add(cohort_new)
        db.session.flush()

        # Create user with scope:dept who is a member of cohort in first subdept
        user = User(username="dept_scope_user", password_hash=hash_password("Test1234!@Ab"), role="faculty_advisor")
        db.session.add(user)
        db.session.flush()
        db.session.add(CohortMember(cohort_id=seeded_assessment["cohort_id"], user_id=user.id, role_in_cohort="faculty_advisor"))
        db.session.add(UserPermission(user_id=user.id, permission="scope:dept"))
        db.session.commit()

        result = rbac_service.resolve_scope(user, {"scope:dept"})
        # Should include cohorts from both sub-departments under the same department
        assert seeded_assessment["cohort_id"] in result
        assert cohort_new.id in result


def test_resolve_scope_subdept(app, seeded_assessment):
    """scope:subdept:<id> resolves to cohorts under that sub-department only."""
    with app.app_context():
        cohort = db.session.get(Cohort, seeded_assessment["cohort_id"])
        klass = db.session.get(Class, cohort.class_id)
        major = db.session.get(Major, klass.major_id)
        subdept_id = major.sub_department_id

        user = User(username="subdept_scope_user", password_hash=hash_password("Test1234!@Ab"), role="faculty_advisor")
        db.session.add(user)
        db.session.flush()
        db.session.add(UserPermission(user_id=user.id, permission=f"scope:subdept:{subdept_id}"))
        db.session.commit()

        result = rbac_service.resolve_scope(user, {f"scope:subdept:{subdept_id}"})
        assert seeded_assessment["cohort_id"] in result
        assert seeded_assessment["cohort2_id"] in result


def test_scope_subdept_grants_cohort_access(app, seeded_assessment):
    """scope:subdept:<id> via UserPermission grants cohort access through can_access_cohort."""
    with app.app_context():
        cohort = db.session.get(Cohort, seeded_assessment["cohort_id"])
        klass = db.session.get(Class, cohort.class_id)
        major = db.session.get(Major, klass.major_id)
        subdept_id = major.sub_department_id

        user = User(username="subdept_access_user", password_hash=hash_password("Test1234!@Ab"), role="faculty_advisor")
        db.session.add(user)
        db.session.flush()
        db.session.add(UserPermission(user_id=user.id, permission=f"scope:subdept:{subdept_id}"))
        db.session.commit()

        assert rbac_service.can_access_cohort(user, seeded_assessment["cohort_id"]) is True


def test_scope_self_grants_own_cohort_access(app, seeded_assessment):
    """scope:self via UserPermission grants access to cohorts user is a member of."""
    with app.app_context():
        user = User(username="self_scope_user", password_hash=hash_password("Test1234!@Ab"), role="faculty_advisor")
        db.session.add(user)
        db.session.flush()
        db.session.add(CohortMember(cohort_id=seeded_assessment["cohort_id"], user_id=user.id, role_in_cohort="faculty_advisor"))
        db.session.add(UserPermission(user_id=user.id, permission="scope:self"))
        db.session.commit()

        assert rbac_service.can_access_cohort(user, seeded_assessment["cohort_id"]) is True
        assert rbac_service.can_access_cohort(user, seeded_assessment["cohort2_id"]) is False


def test_scope_dept_denies_cross_department(app, seeded_assessment):
    """scope:dept must NOT grant access to cohorts in a different department."""
    with app.app_context():
        # Create a totally separate department/school/cohort
        school2 = School(name="Other School", code="OS", is_active=True)
        db.session.add(school2)
        db.session.flush()
        dept2 = Department(school_id=school2.id, name="Other Dept", code="OD")
        db.session.add(dept2)
        db.session.flush()
        sub2 = SubDepartment(department_id=dept2.id, name="Other Sub", code="OSUB")
        db.session.add(sub2)
        db.session.flush()
        major2 = Major(name="Other Major", code="OM", school_id=school2.id, sub_department_id=sub2.id)
        db.session.add(major2)
        db.session.flush()
        class2 = Class(name="Other Class", year=2026, major_id=major2.id)
        db.session.add(class2)
        db.session.flush()
        other_cohort = Cohort(name="Other Cohort", class_id=class2.id, internship_term="2026 Spring", is_active=True)
        db.session.add(other_cohort)
        db.session.flush()

        user = User(username="cross_dept_user", password_hash=hash_password("Test1234!@Ab"), role="faculty_advisor")
        db.session.add(user)
        db.session.flush()
        # User is a member of seeded cohort (first dept)
        db.session.add(CohortMember(cohort_id=seeded_assessment["cohort_id"], user_id=user.id, role_in_cohort="faculty_advisor"))
        db.session.add(UserPermission(user_id=user.id, permission="scope:dept"))
        db.session.commit()

        # Should access own department's cohort
        assert rbac_service.can_access_cohort(user, seeded_assessment["cohort_id"]) is True
        # Should NOT access other department's cohort
        assert rbac_service.can_access_cohort(user, other_cohort.id) is False


def test_resolve_scope_empty_scopes_returns_empty(app, seeded_assessment):
    """Empty scope set returns no cohort IDs."""
    with app.app_context():
        user = User.query.filter_by(username="advisor1").first()
        result = rbac_service.resolve_scope(user, set())
        assert result == set()
"}

            role="faculty_advisor",
        )
        db.session.add(user)
        db.session.flush()

        delegation = TemporaryDelegation(

            delegate_id=user.id,

        assert rbac_service.can_access_cohort(user, seeded_assessment["cohort_id"]) is True", "new_string": "def test_delegation_scope_global_allows_all_cohorts(app, seeded_assessment):
    with app.app_context():
        user = User(
            username="deleg_scope_global",

            role="faculty_advisor",
        )
        db.session.add(user)
        db.session.flush()

        delegation = TemporaryDelegation(
            delegator_id=seeded_assessment["admin_id"],
            delegate_id=user.id,
            scope="scope:global",
            permissions='["cohort:view"]',
            expires_at=datetime.now(timezone.utc).replace(tzinfo=None) + timedelta(days=1),
            is_active=True,
        )
        db.session.add(delegation)
        db.session.commit()

        assert rbac_service.can_access_cohort(user, seeded_assessment["cohort_id"]) is True


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
#  resolve_scope tests
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------


def test_resolve_scope_global_returns_all(app, seeded_assessment):
    """scope:global resolves to every active cohort."""
    with app.app_context():
        all_cohorts = Cohort.query.filter(Cohort.is_active.is_(True)).all()
        user = User.query.filter_by(username="advisor1").first()
        result = rbac_service.resolve_scope(user, {"scope:global"})
        assert result == {c.id for c in all_cohorts}


def test_resolve_scope_self_returns_member_cohorts(app, seeded_assessment):
    """scope:self resolves to only the cohorts where the user is a member."""
    with app.app_context():
        student = User.query.filter_by(username="student1").first()
        result = rbac_service.resolve_scope(student, {"scope:self"})
        assert result == {seeded_assessment["cohort_id"]}


def test_resolve_scope_cohort_returns_specific(app, seeded_assessment):
    """scope:cohort:<id> resolves to just that cohort."""
    with app.app_context():
        user = User.query.filter_by(username="advisor1").first()
        cid = seeded_assessment["cohort_id"]
        result = rbac_service.resolve_scope(user, {f"scope:cohort:{cid}"})
        assert result == {cid}


def test_resolve_scope_dept_includes_subdepartments(app, seeded_assessment):
    """scope:dept resolves to all cohorts under the user's department tree."""
    with app.app_context():
        cohort = db.session.get(Cohort, seeded_assessment["cohort_id"])
        klass = db.session.get(Class, cohort.class_id)
        major = db.session.get(Major, klass.major_id)
        subdept = db.session.get(SubDepartment, major.sub_department_id)
        dept = db.session.get(Department, subdept.department_id)

        sub2 = SubDepartment(department_id=dept.id, name="Sub2", code="S2")
        db.session.add(sub2)
        db.session.flush()
        school = db.session.get(School, major.school_id)
        major2 = Major(name="Major2", code="M2", school_id=school.id, sub_department_id=sub2.id)
        db.session.add(major2)
        db.session.flush()
        class2 = Class(name="Class2", year=2026, major_id=major2.id)
        db.session.add(class2)
        db.session.flush()
        cohort_new = Cohort(name="Cohort Dept2", class_id=class2.id, internship_term="2026 Spring", is_active=True)
        db.session.add(cohort_new)
        db.session.flush()

        user = User(username="dept_scope_user", password_hash=hash_password("Test1234!@Ab"), role="faculty_advisor")
        db.session.add(user)
        db.session.flush()
        db.session.add(CohortMember(cohort_id=seeded_assessment["cohort_id"], user_id=user.id, role_in_cohort="faculty_advisor"))
        db.session.add(UserPermission(user_id=user.id, permission="scope:dept"))
        db.session.commit()

        result = rbac_service.resolve_scope(user, {"scope:dept"})
        assert seeded_assessment["cohort_id"] in result
        assert cohort_new.id in result


def test_resolve_scope_subdept(app, seeded_assessment):
    """scope:subdept:<id> resolves to cohorts under that sub-department only."""
    with app.app_context():
        cohort = db.session.get(Cohort, seeded_assessment["cohort_id"])
        klass = db.session.get(Class, cohort.class_id)
        major = db.session.get(Major, klass.major_id)
        subdept_id = major.sub_department_id

        user = User(username="subdept_scope_user", password_hash=hash_password("Test1234!@Ab"), role="faculty_advisor")
        db.session.add(user)
        db.session.flush()
        db.session.add(UserPermission(user_id=user.id, permission=f"scope:subdept:{subdept_id}"))
        db.session.commit()

        result = rbac_service.resolve_scope(user, {f"scope:subdept:{subdept_id}"})
        assert seeded_assessment["cohort_id"] in result
        assert seeded_assessment["cohort2_id"] in result


def test_scope_subdept_grants_cohort_access(app, seeded_assessment):
    """scope:subdept:<id> via UserPermission grants cohort access through can_access_cohort."""
    with app.app_context():
        cohort = db.session.get(Cohort, seeded_assessment["cohort_id"])
        klass = db.session.get(Class, cohort.class_id)
        major = db.session.get(Major, klass.major_id)
        subdept_id = major.sub_department_id

        user = User(username="subdept_access_user", password_hash=hash_password("Test1234!@Ab"), role="faculty_advisor")
        db.session.add(user)
        db.session.flush()
        db.session.add(UserPermission(user_id=user.id, permission=f"scope:subdept:{subdept_id}"))
        db.session.commit()

        assert rbac_service.can_access_cohort(user, seeded_assessment["cohort_id"]) is True


def test_scope_self_grants_own_cohort_access(app, seeded_assessment):
    """scope:self via UserPermission grants access to cohorts user is a member of."""
    with app.app_context():
        user = User(username="self_scope_user", password_hash=hash_password("Test1234!@Ab"), role="faculty_advisor")
        db.session.add(user)
        db.session.flush()
        db.session.add(CohortMember(cohort_id=seeded_assessment["cohort_id"], user_id=user.id, role_in_cohort="faculty_advisor"))
        db.session.add(UserPermission(user_id=user.id, permission="scope:self"))
        db.session.commit()

        assert rbac_service.can_access_cohort(user, seeded_assessment["cohort_id"]) is True
        assert rbac_service.can_access_cohort(user, seeded_assessment["cohort2_id"]) is False


def test_scope_dept_denies_cross_department(app, seeded_assessment):
    """scope:dept must NOT grant access to cohorts in a different department."""
    with app.app_context():
        school2 = School(name="Other School", code="OS", is_active=True)
        db.session.add(school2)
        db.session.flush()
        dept2 = Department(school_id=school2.id, name="Other Dept", code="OD")
        db.session.add(dept2)
        db.session.flush()
        sub2 = SubDepartment(department_id=dept2.id, name="Other Sub", code="OSUB")
        db.session.add(sub2)
        db.session.flush()
        major2 = Major(name="Other Major", code="OM", school_id=school2.id, sub_department_id=sub2.id)
        db.session.add(major2)
        db.session.flush()
        class2 = Class(name="Other Class", year=2026, major_id=major2.id)
        db.session.add(class2)
        db.session.flush()
        other_cohort = Cohort(name="Other Cohort", class_id=class2.id, internship_term="2026 Spring", is_active=True)
        db.session.add(other_cohort)
        db.session.flush()

        user = User(username="cross_dept_user", password_hash=hash_password("Test1234!@Ab"), role="faculty_advisor")
        db.session.add(user)
        db.session.flush()
        db.session.add(CohortMember(cohort_id=seeded_assessment["cohort_id"], user_id=user.id, role_in_cohort="faculty_advisor"))
        db.session.add(UserPermission(user_id=user.id, permission="scope:dept"))
        db.session.commit()

        assert rbac_service.can_access_cohort(user, seeded_assessment["cohort_id"]) is True
        assert rbac_service.can_access_cohort(user, other_cohort.id) is False


def test_resolve_scope_empty_scopes_returns_empty(app, seeded_assessment):
    """Empty scope set returns no cohort IDs."""
    with app.app_context():
        user = User.query.filter_by(username="advisor1").first()
        result = rbac_service.resolve_scope(user, set())
        assert result == set()"}

import secrets
from datetime import timedelta
from pathlib import Path

DEFAULT_DATABASE_URL = "sqlite:///data/practicum.db"

# Known-weak patterns that must never be used outside tests.
_INSECURE_KEY_PATTERNS = (
    "practicum-dev-secret-key-change-in-production",
    "change-me-in-production",
    "change-me",
    "secret",
)


def _get_or_generate_secret_key() -> str:
    """Return a safe SECRET_KEY for non-test runtime.

    Priority:
    1. ``SECRET_KEY`` environment variable (must not be a known-weak value).
    2. A per-install random key persisted to ``data/.secret_key``.
    3. Generates a new random key and persists it.
    """
    env_key = os.environ.get("SECRET_KEY", "").strip()

    if env_key:
        low = env_key.lower()
        if any(pat in low for pat in _INSECURE_KEY_PATTERNS):
            # Fall through to auto-generated key rather than using weak value.
            pass
        elif len(env_key) < 16:
            pass  # Too short — fall through.
        else:
            return env_key

    # Auto-generate and persist a per-install secret.
    key_path = Path(os.environ.get("SECRET_KEY_FILE", "data/.secret_key"))
    if not key_path.is_absolute():
        key_path = Path(__file__).resolve().parent.parent / key_path
    try:
        existing = key_path.read_text().strip()
        if len(existing) >= 32:
            return existing
    except (FileNotFoundError, PermissionError, OSError):
        pass

    new_key = secrets.token_hex(32)
    try:
        key_path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
        key_path.write_text(new_key)
        key_path.chmod(0o600)
    except OSError:
        pass  # Ephemeral environments (e.g. CI): use in-memory key.
    return new_key


class Config:
    SECRET_KEY = _get_or_generate_secret_key()
    WTF_CSRF_SECRET_KEY = os.environ.get("WTF_CSRF_SECRET_KEY", SECRET_KEY)
    SQLALCHEMY_DATABASE_URI = os.environ.get("DATABASE_URL", DEFAULT_DATABASE_URL)
    SQLALCHEMY_TRACK_MODIFICATIONS = False
    SESSION_TYPE = "filesystem"
    PERMANENT_SESSION_LIFETIME = timedelta(minutes=30)


class DevelopmentConfig(Config):
    DEBUG = True


class TestingConfig(Config):
    TESTING = True
    SECRET_KEY = os.environ.get("SECRET_KEY", "testing-secret-key")
    WTF_CSRF_SECRET_KEY = os.environ.get("WTF_CSRF_SECRET_KEY", SECRET_KEY)
    SQLALCHEMY_DATABASE_URI = "sqlite:///:memory:"
    WTF_CSRF_ENABLED = False


class ProductionConfig(Config):
    DEBUG = False


config_by_name = {
    "development": DevelopmentConfig,
    "testing": TestingConfig,
    "production": ProductionConfig,
}"}

        elif len(env_key) < 16:
            pass  # Too short — fall through.
        else:
            return env_key

    # Auto-generate and persist a per-install secret.
    key_path = Path(os.environ.get("SECRET_KEY_FILE", "data/.secret_key"))
    if not key_path.is_absolute():
        key_path = Path(__file__).resolve().parent.parent / key_path
    try:
        existing = key_path.read_text().strip()
        if len(existing) >= 32:
            return existing
    except (FileNotFoundError, PermissionError, OSError):
        pass

    new_key = secrets.token_hex(32)
    try:
        key_path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
        key_path.write_text(new_key)
        key_path.chmod(0o600)
    except OSError:
        pass  # Ephemeral environments (e.g. CI): use in-memory key.
    return new_key

    SECRET_KEY = _get_or_generate_secret_key()
    WTF_CSRF_SECRET_KEY = os.environ.get("WTF_CSRF_SECRET_KEY", SECRET_KEY)
    SQLALCHEMY_DATABASE_URI = os.environ.get("DATABASE_URL", DEFAULT_DATABASE_URL)
    SQLALCHEMY_TRACK_MODIFICATIONS = False
    SESSION_TYPE = "filesystem"
    PERMANENT_SESSION_LIFETIME = timedelta(minutes=30)


class DevelopmentConfig(Config):
    DEBUG = True


class TestingConfig(Config):
    TESTING = True
    SECRET_KEY = os.environ.get("SECRET_KEY", "testing-secret-key")
    WTF_CSRF_SECRET_KEY = os.environ.get("WTF_CSRF_SECRET_KEY", SECRET_KEY)
    SQLALCHEMY_DATABASE_URI = "sqlite:///:memory:"
    WTF_CSRF_ENABLED = False


class ProductionConfig(Config):
    DEBUG = False

    "development": DevelopmentConfig,
    "testing": TestingConfig,
    "production": ProductionConfig,
}"}

import secrets
from datetime import timedelta
from pathlib import Path

DEFAULT_DATABASE_URL = "sqlite:///data/practicum.db"

# Known-weak patterns that must never be used outside tests.
_INSECURE_KEY_PATTERNS = (
    "practicum-dev-secret-key-change-in-production",
    "change-me-in-production",
    "change-me",
    "secret",
)


def _get_or_generate_secret_key() -> str:
    """Return a safe SECRET_KEY for non-test runtime.

    Priority:
    1. ``SECRET_KEY`` environment variable (must not be a known-weak value).
    2. A per-install random key persisted to ``data/.secret_key``.
    3. Generates a new random key and persists it.
    """
    env_key = os.environ.get("SECRET_KEY", "").strip()

    if env_key:
        low = env_key.lower()
        if any(pat in low for pat in _INSECURE_KEY_PATTERNS):
            # Fall through to auto-generated key rather than using weak value.
            pass
        elif len(env_key) < 16:
            pass  # Too short — fall through.
        else:
            return env_key

    # Auto-generate and persist a per-install secret.
    key_path = Path(os.environ.get("SECRET_KEY_FILE", "data/.secret_key"))
    if not key_path.is_absolute():
        key_path = Path(__file__).resolve().parent.parent / key_path
    try:
        existing = key_path.read_text().strip()
        if len(existing) >= 32:
            return existing
    except (FileNotFoundError, PermissionError, OSError):
        pass

    new_key = secrets.token_hex(32)
    try:
        key_path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
        key_path.write_text(new_key)
        key_path.chmod(0o600)
    except OSError:
        pass  # Ephemeral environments (e.g. CI): use in-memory key.
    return new_key


class Config:
    SECRET_KEY = _get_or_generate_secret_key()
    WTF_CSRF_SECRET_KEY = os.environ.get("WTF_CSRF_SECRET_KEY", SECRET_KEY)
    SQLALCHEMY_DATABASE_URI = os.environ.get("DATABASE_URL", DEFAULT_DATABASE_URL)
    SQLALCHEMY_TRACK_MODIFICATIONS = False
    SESSION_TYPE = "filesystem"
    PERMANENT_SESSION_LIFETIME = timedelta(minutes=30)


class DevelopmentConfig(Config):
    DEBUG = True


class TestingConfig(Config):
    TESTING = True
    SECRET_KEY = os.environ.get("SECRET_KEY", "testing-secret-key")
    WTF_CSRF_SECRET_KEY = os.environ.get("WTF_CSRF_SECRET_KEY", SECRET_KEY)
    SQLALCHEMY_DATABASE_URI = "sqlite:///:memory:"
    WTF_CSRF_ENABLED = False


class ProductionConfig(Config):
    DEBUG = False


config_by_name = {
    "development": DevelopmentConfig,
    "testing": TestingConfig,
    "production": ProductionConfig,
}"}

{"file_path": "/Users/apple/Documents/projects/eagle-point_new/Practicum-Assessment/repo/tests/unit/test_secret_key.py", "content": """"Tests for SECRET_KEY security (F-002)."""
import os
import tempfile

import pytest


def test_testing_config_uses_deterministic_key():
    """TestingConfig must use a deterministic key so tests are reproducible."""
    from app.config import TestingConfig

    assert TestingConfig.SECRET_KEY == "testing-secret-key"


def test_weak_key_rejected_by_generator(monkeypatch, tmp_path):
    """_get_or_generate_secret_key must reject known-weak patterns."""
    monkeypatch.setenv("SECRET_KEY", "practicum-dev-secret-key-change-in-production")
    key_file = tmp_path / ".secret_key"
    monkeypatch.setenv("SECRET_KEY_FILE", str(key_file))
    # Re-import to pick up changed env
    from app.config import _get_or_generate_secret_key

    result = _get_or_generate_secret_key()
    assert result != "practicum-dev-secret-key-change-in-production"
    assert len(result) >= 32


def test_short_key_rejected(monkeypatch, tmp_path):
    """Keys shorter than 16 chars are rejected."""
    monkeypatch.setenv("SECRET_KEY", "short")
    key_file = tmp_path / ".secret_key"
    monkeypatch.setenv("SECRET_KEY_FILE", str(key_file))
    from app.config import _get_or_generate_secret_key

    result = _get_or_generate_secret_key()
    assert result != "short"
    assert len(result) >= 32


def test_strong_env_key_accepted(monkeypatch, tmp_path):
    """A strong env key is used as-is."""
    strong = "a" * 64
    monkeypatch.setenv("SECRET_KEY", strong)
    monkeypatch.setenv("SECRET_KEY_FILE", str(tmp_path / ".secret_key"))
    from app.config import _get_or_generate_secret_key

    assert _get_or_generate_secret_key() == strong


def test_auto_generated_key_persisted(monkeypatch, tmp_path):
    """When no env key, a random key is generated and persisted to file."""
    monkeypatch.setenv("SECRET_KEY", "")
    key_file = tmp_path / ".secret_key"
    monkeypatch.setenv("SECRET_KEY_FILE", str(key_file))
    from app.config import _get_or_generate_secret_key

    key1 = _get_or_generate_secret_key()
    assert len(key1) >= 32
    assert key_file.exists()
    # Second call reads from file — same key
    key2 = _get_or_generate_secret_key()
    assert key1 == key2


def test_config_secret_key_not_default():
    """The non-test Config class must never use the old hardcoded default."""
    from app.config import Config

    assert "practicum-dev-secret-key" not in Config.SECRET_KEY
    assert "change-me" not in Config.SECRET_KEY.lower()
    assert len(Config.SECRET_KEY) >= 16
"}

    assert result != "practicum-dev-secret-key-change-in-production"
    assert len(result) >= 32


def test_short_key_rejected(monkeypatch, tmp_path):
    """Keys shorter than 16 chars are rejected."""
    monkeypatch.setenv("SECRET_KEY", "short")
    key_file = tmp_path / ".secret_key"
    monkeypatch.setenv("SECRET_KEY_FILE", str(key_file))
    from app.config import _get_or_generate_secret_key

    result = _get_or_generate_secret_key()
    assert result != "short"
    assert len(result) >= 32


def test_strong_env_key_accepted(monkeypatch, tmp_path):
    """A strong env key is used as-is."""
    strong = "a" * 64
    monkeypatch.setenv("SECRET_KEY", strong)
    monkeypatch.setenv("SECRET_KEY_FILE", str(tmp_path / ".secret_key"))
    from app.config import _get_or_generate_secret_key

    assert _get_or_generate_secret_key() == strong


def test_auto_generated_key_persisted(monkeypatch, tmp_path):
    """When no env key, a random key is generated and persisted to file."""
    monkeypatch.setenv("SECRET_KEY", "")
    key_file = tmp_path / ".secret_key"
    monkeypatch.setenv("SECRET_KEY_FILE", str(key_file))
    from app.config import _get_or_generate_secret_key

    key1 = _get_or_generate_secret_key()
    assert len(key1) >= 32
    assert key_file.exists()
    # Second call reads from file — same key
    key2 = _get_or_generate_secret_key()
    assert key1 == key2


def test_config_secret_key_not_default():
    """The non-test Config class must never use the old hardcoded default."""
    from app.config import Config

    assert "practicum-dev-secret-key" not in Config.SECRET_KEY
    assert "change-me" not in Config.SECRET_KEY.lower()
    assert len(Config.SECRET_KEY) >= 16
"}

    """Run anomaly detection for a single user and persist new flags.

    from app.models.anomaly_flag import AnomalyFlag

    for message in anomaly_messages:

        ).first()

                anomaly_type=message,
                detected_at=datetime.now(timezone.utc).replace(tzinfo=None),
                reviewed=False,
            )

    if created:

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
#  evaluate_user_anomalies (automatic on-login trigger) tests
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------


def test_evaluate_user_anomalies_creates_flags(app):
    """evaluate_user_anomalies persists AnomalyFlag rows without manual scan."""
    from app.models.anomaly_flag import AnomalyFlag

    uid = _make_user(app, "auto_flag_user")
    now = _utcnow()
    # Trigger brute-force condition: >5 failures in 1h
    for _ in range(6):

## F-004: Audit Immutability — Archive Before Purge

The current `purge_old_logs()` does a raw SQL delete bypassing the ORM event listener. I'll implement Option 1: archive logs to CSV before purging, and enforce append-only within the retention window.

## F-004: Audit Immutability — Archive Before Purge

The current `purge_old_logs()` does a raw SQL delete bypassing the ORM event listener. I'll implement Option 1: archive logs to CSV before purging, and enforce append-only within the retention window.

{"replace_all": false, "file_path": "/Users/apple/Documents/projects/eagle-point_new/Practicum-Assessment/repo/app/services/audit_service.py", "old_string": "def purge_old_logs() -> int:
    """Delete audit logs older than AUDIT_RETENTION_DAYS. Returns count deleted."""
    cutoff = datetime.now(timezone.utc).replace(tzinfo=None) - timedelta(days=AUDIT_RETENTION_DAYS)
    query = AuditLog.query.filter(AuditLog.created_at < cutoff)
    count = query.count()
    if count == 0:
        return 0
    query.delete(synchronize_session=False)
    db.session.commit()
    return count", "new_string": "def _archive_logs(logs: list[AuditLog], archive_dir: str | None = None) -> str | None:
    """Write *logs* to a timestamped CSV archive file.

    Returns the archive file path, or ``None`` if no logs to archive.
    """
    if not logs:
        return None

    if archive_dir is None:
        archive_dir = os.environ.get("AUDIT_ARCHIVE_DIR", "data/audit_archive")

    from pathlib import Path

    path = Path(archive_dir)
    path.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)

    stamp = datetime.now(timezone.utc).strftime("%Y%m%dT%H%M%SZ")
    filepath = path / f"audit_archive_{stamp}.csv"

    buffer = StringIO()
    writer = csv.writer(buffer)
    writer.writerow([
        "id", "timestamp", "actor_id", "actor_username", "action",
        "resource_type", "resource_id", "old_value", "new_value",
        "ip_address", "device_fingerprint", "extra",
    ])
    for row in logs:
        writer.writerow([
            row.id,
            row.created_at.isoformat() if row.created_at else "",
            row.actor_id or "",
            row.actor_username or "",
            row.action,
            row.resource_type or "",
            row.resource_id or "",
            row.old_value or "",
            row.new_value or "",
            row.ip_address or "",
            row.device_fingerprint or "",
            row.extra or "",
        ])

    filepath.write_text(buffer.getvalue(), encoding="utf-8")
    return str(filepath)


def purge_old_logs() -> int:
    """Archive then delete audit logs older than AUDIT_RETENTION_DAYS.

    Append-only semantics are enforced within the retention window by the
    ORM-level event listeners on AuditLog (before_update / before_delete).
    Beyond the retention window, logs are first archived to CSV, then bulk-
    deleted via a raw query that bypasses the ORM listeners.

    Returns count of purged rows.
    """
    cutoff = datetime.now(timezone.utc).replace(tzinfo=None) - timedelta(days=AUDIT_RETENTION_DAYS)
    expired = AuditLog.query.filter(AuditLog.created_at < cutoff).all()
    if not expired:
        return 0

    # Archive before delete.
    _archive_logs(expired)

    count = len(expired)
    expired_ids = [row.id for row in expired]
    # Bulk delete via raw SQL to bypass ORM before_delete listener (which
    # enforces append-only within the retention window).
    from sqlalchemy import text

    db.session.execute(
        text("DELETE FROM audit_logs WHERE id IN :ids"),
        {"ids": tuple(expired_ids)},
    )
    db.session.commit()
    return count"}

            row.created_at.isoformat() if row.created_at else "",
            row.actor_id or "",
            row.actor_username or "",
            row.action,
            row.resource_type or "",
            row.resource_id or "",
            row.old_value or "",
            row.new_value or "",
            row.ip_address or "",
            row.device_fingerprint or "",
            row.extra or "",
        ])

    filepath.write_text(buffer.getvalue(), encoding="utf-8")
    return str(filepath)


def purge_old_logs() -> int:
    """Archive then delete audit logs older than AUDIT_RETENTION_DAYS.

    Append-only semantics are enforced within the retention window by the
    ORM-level event listeners on AuditLog (before_update / before_delete).
    Beyond the retention window, logs are first archived to CSV, then bulk-
    deleted via a raw query that bypasses the ORM listeners.

    Returns count of purged rows.
    """
    cutoff = datetime.now(timezone.utc).replace(tzinfo=None) - timedelta(days=AUDIT_RETENTION_DAYS)
    expired = AuditLog.query.filter(AuditLog.created_at < cutoff).all()
    if not expired:
        return 0

    # Archive before delete.
    _archive_logs(expired)

    count = len(expired)
    expired_ids = [row.id for row in expired]
    # Bulk delete via raw SQL to bypass ORM before_delete listener (which
    # enforces append-only within the retention window).
    from sqlalchemy import text

    db.session.execute(
        text("DELETE FROM audit_logs WHERE id IN :ids"),
        {"ids": tuple(expired_ids)},
    )
    db.session.commit()
    return count"}

        keep_row = AuditLog(action="NEW", created_at=datetime.now(timezone.utc).replace(tzinfo=None) - timedelta(days=5))
        db.session.add_all([old_row, keep_row])
        db.session.commit()

        deleted = audit_service.purge_old_logs()

        assert deleted == 1
        assert AuditLog.query.filter_by(action="OLD").count() == 0
        assert AuditLog.query.filter_by(action="NEW").count() == 1


def test_purge_archives_before_delete(app, tmp_path, monkeypatch):
    """purge_old_logs must create a CSV archive file before deleting rows."""
    monkeypatch.setenv("AUDIT_ARCHIVE_DIR", str(tmp_path / "archive"))
    with app.app_context():
        old_row = AuditLog(
            action="ARCHIVE_ME",
            actor_username="test_actor",
            created_at=datetime.now(timezone.utc).replace(tzinfo=None) - timedelta(days=1200),
        )
        db.session.add(old_row)
        db.session.commit()

        deleted = audit_service.purge_old_logs()
        assert deleted == 1

        archive_dir = tmp_path / "archive"
        assert archive_dir.exists()
        csv_files = list(archive_dir.glob("audit_archive_*.csv"))
        assert len(csv_files) >= 1
        content = csv_files[0].read_text()
        assert "ARCHIVE_ME" in content
        assert "test_actor" in content


def test_purge_no_expired_returns_zero(app):
    """If no logs are expired, purge returns 0 and does nothing."""
    with app.app_context():
        fresh = AuditLog(action="FRESH", created_at=datetime.now(timezone.utc).replace(tzinfo=None))
        db.session.add(fresh)
        db.session.commit()
        assert audit_service.purge_old_logs() == 0
        assert AuditLog.query.filter_by(action="FRESH").count() == 1


def test_append_only_within_retention_window(app):
    """Logs within the retention window cannot be updated or deleted via ORM."""
    with app.app_context():
        log = AuditLog(
            action="RECENT_LOG",
            created_at=datetime.now(timezone.utc).replace(tzinfo=None) - timedelta(days=5),
        )
        db.session.add(log)
        db.session.commit()

        # ORM update must raise
        log.action = "TAMPERED"
        import sqlalchemy.exc
        with pytest.raises((PermissionError, sqlalchemy.exc.InvalidRequestError)):
            db.session.flush()
        db.session.rollback()

        # ORM delete must raise
        fetched = db.session.get(AuditLog, log.id)
        with pytest.raises(PermissionError):
            fetched.delete()


def test_audit_log_immut_update_raises_permission_error(app):
    """Direct ORM update of an AuditLog must raise PermissionError."""
    with app.app_context():
        log = AuditLog(action="IMMUTABLE_TEST")
        db.session.add(log)
        db.session.commit()
        log_id = log.id

        fetched = db.session.get(AuditLog, log_id)

        with pytest.raises(PermissionError):
            fetched.update(action="TAMPERED")


def test_audit_log_immut_delete_raises_permission_error(app):
    """Direct ORM delete of an AuditLog must raise PermissionError."""
    with app.app_context():
        log = AuditLog(action="DELETE_TEST")
        db.session.add(log)
        db.session.commit()
        log_id = log.id
        fetched = db.session.get(AuditLog, log_id)

        with pytest.raises(PermissionError):
            fetched.delete()


def test_audit_log_immut_sqlalchemy_before_update_hook_fires(app):
    """SQLAlchemy before_update event must prevent UPDATE reaching the DB."""
    with app.app_context():
        import sqlalchemy.exc

        log = AuditLog(action="HOOK_TEST")
        db.session.add(log)
        db.session.commit()

        log.action = "MODIFIED"
        with pytest.raises((PermissionError, sqlalchemy.exc.InvalidRequestError)):
            db.session.flush()
        db.session.rollback()"}

## Final Output

### 1) Files Changed Grouped by Issue

**A) F-001 — Hierarchical Scope Model**
- `app/services/rbac_service.py` — Added `resolve_scope()`, `_get_subdept_cohort_ids()`, `_get_dept_and_subdept_cohort_ids()`, `SCOPE_LEVELS`, `scope:subdept:<id>` and `scope:self` support in `_scope_permits_cohort()` and `get_accessible_cohorts()`
- `docs/questions.md` — Updated to document complete scope hierarchy (was "not fully implemented", now documents all 8 levels)
- `tests/unit/test_rbac_service.py` — Added 9 new tests

**B) F-002 — Predictable Default Secret Key**
- `app/config.py` — Replaced hardcoded `DEFAULT_SECRET_KEY` with `_get_or_generate_secret_key()` that rejects weak keys, auto-generates and persists per-install random key
- `docker-compose.yml` — Removed weak default (`change-me-in-production-min-32-chars` → empty)
- `run_tests.sh` — Changed fallback key to test-only value
- `README.md` — Added SECRET_KEY generation instructions
- `tests/unit/test_secret_key.py` — New file with 6 tests

**C) F-003 — Automatic Anomaly Detection**
- `app/services/audit_service.py` — Added `evaluate_user_anomalies()` for incremental per-user anomaly evaluation
- `app/services/auth_service.py` — Called `evaluate_user_anomalies()` on successful login and failed login (brute force detection)
- `app/routes/auth.py` — Called `evaluate_user_anomalies()` after MFA login
- `tests/unit/test_anomaly_detection.py` — Added 4 new tests

**D) F-004 — Audit Immutability / Archive Before Purge**
- `app/services/audit_service.py` — Rewrote `purge_old_logs()` to archive to CSV before deleting; added `_archive_logs()` helper
- `tests/unit/test_audit_service.py` — Added 3 new tests (archive verification, append-only enforcement, no-expired handling)

### 2) What Changed and Why

- **F-001**: The scope model was documented as "not fully implemented". Added `resolve_scope()` as a deterministic policy engine that converts any set of scope strings into concrete cohort IDs. Added `scope:self` (member-only access) and `scope:subdept:<id>` (sub-department level). All existing scope checks (`can_access_cohort`, `get_accessible_cohorts`) now handle the full hierarchy consistently.

- **F-002**: The old `Config` class had a hardcoded `DEFAULT_SECRET_KEY` used in production. Now `_get_or_generate_secret_key()` rejects known-weak patterns and short keys, auto-generates a per-install 64-char hex key persisted to `data/.secret_key`, and only `TestingConfig` retains a deterministic key.

- **F-003**: Anomaly detection was manual-scan-only via `POST /admin/anomalies/scan`. Added `evaluate_user_anomalies(user_id, username)` which runs incrementally for one user at a time on login/failed-login events. No full-table scan, de-duplicates against existing unreviewed flags.

- **F-004**: `purge_old_logs()` previously did a raw delete without archiving, creating immutability ambiguity. Now archives to timestamped CSV files in `data/audit_archive/` before deleting. ORM-level `before_update`/`before_delete` listeners enforce append-only within the retention window; only the policy purge bypasses via raw SQL after archiving.

### 3) New Test Cases

| Test Name | Assertion Intent |
|-----------|-----------------|
| `test_resolve_scope_global_returns_all` | scope:global returns every active cohort ID |
| `test_resolve_scope_self_returns_member_cohorts` | scope:self returns only member cohorts |
| `test_resolve_scope_cohort_returns_specific` | scope:cohort:<id> returns exactly that ID |
| `test_resolve_scope_dept_includes_subdepartments` | scope:dept includes cohorts across all sub-depts of user's dept |
| `test_resolve_scope_subdept` | scope:subdept:<id> returns cohorts under that sub-department |
| `test_scope_subdept_grants_cohort_access` | scope:subdept via UserPermission grants access through can_access_cohort |
| `test_scope_self_grants_own_cohort_access` | scope:self allows own cohort, denies other |
| `test_scope_dept_denies_cross_department` | scope:dept does NOT grant cross-department access |
| `test_resolve_scope_empty_scopes_returns_empty` | Empty scope set returns empty set |
| `test_testing_config_uses_deterministic_key` | Test config has reproducible key |
| `test_weak_key_rejected_by_generator` | Known-weak patterns are rejected |
| `test_short_key_rejected` | Keys < 16 chars rejected |
| `test_strong_env_key_accepted` | Strong env key used as-is |
| `test_auto_generated_key_persisted` | Auto-generated key persisted to file and reused |
| `test_config_secret_key_not_default` | Config class never uses old hardcoded default |
| `test_evaluate_user_anomalies_creates_flags` | evaluate_user_anomalies persists AnomalyFlag rows |
| `test_evaluate_user_anomalies_deduplicates` | Second call creates no duplicate flags |
| `test_evaluate_user_anomalies_no_anomaly_no_flags` | No anomalies = no flags created |
| `test_login_triggers_anomaly_evaluation` | Real login triggers automatic anomaly evaluation |
| `test_purge_archives_before_delete` | Purge creates CSV archive before deleting |
| `test_purge_no_expired_returns_zero` | No expired logs = 0 deleted, nothing archived |
| `test_append_only_within_retention_window` | ORM update/delete blocked for recent logs |

### 4) Test Run Results

```

E2E tests:    14 passed, 0 failed

### 5) Assumptions

- **F-001**: `scope:dept` uses the user's `CohortMember` rows to infer their department (via major → sub_department → department). This is the existing pattern; no new user-to-department mapping table was added.
- **F-002**: Auto-generated keys are persisted to `data/.secret_key` (configurable via `SECRET_KEY_FILE` env var). If filesystem is read-only (e.g. ephemeral CI), the key exists only in-memory for that session.
- **F-003**: Anomaly evaluation on login is lightweight (single-user queries only). The manual scan endpoint (`POST /admin/anomalies/scan`) is preserved for bulk admin review.
- **F-004**: Archive files are stored in `data/audit_archive/` (configurable via `AUDIT_ARCHIVE_DIR`). The raw SQL delete bypasses ORM listeners intentionally — this is the only authorized deletion path, and only after archiving.

Now `_get_or_generate_secret_key()` rejects known-weak patterns and short keys, auto-generates a per-install 64-char hex key persisted to `data/.secret_key`, and only `TestingConfig` retains a deterministic key.

- **F-003**: Anomaly detection was manual-scan-only via `POST /admin/anomalies/scan`. Added `evaluate_user_anomalies(user_id, username)` which runs incrementally for one user at a time on login/failed-login events. No full-table scan, de-duplicates against existing unreviewed flags.

- **F-004**: `purge_old_logs()` previously did a raw delete without archiving, creating immutability ambiguity. Now archives to timestamped CSV files in `data/audit_archive/` before deleting. ORM-level `before_update`/`before_delete` listeners enforce append-only within the retention window; only the policy purge bypasses via raw SQL after archiving.

### 3) New Test Cases

| Test Name | Assertion Intent |
|-----------|-----------------|
| `test_resolve_scope_global_returns_all` | scope:global returns every active cohort ID |
| `test_resolve_scope_self_returns_member_cohorts` | scope:self returns only member cohorts |
| `test_resolve_scope_cohort_returns_specific` | scope:cohort:<id> returns exactly that ID |
| `test_resolve_scope_dept_includes_subdepartments` | scope:dept includes cohorts across all sub-depts of user's dept |
| `test_resolve_scope_subdept` | scope:subdept:<id> returns cohorts under that sub-department |
| `test_scope_subdept_grants_cohort_access` | scope:subdept via UserPermission grants access through can_access_cohort |
| `test_scope_self_grants_own_cohort_access` | scope:self allows own cohort, denies other |
| `test_scope_dept_denies_cross_department` | scope:dept does NOT grant cross-department access |
| `test_resolve_scope_empty_scopes_returns_empty` | Empty scope set returns empty set |
| `test_testing_config_uses_deterministic_key` | Test config has reproducible key |
| `test_weak_key_rejected_by_generator` | Known-weak patterns are rejected |
| `test_short_key_rejected` | Keys < 16 chars rejected |
| `test_strong_env_key_accepted` | Strong env key used as-is |
| `test_auto_generated_key_persisted` | Auto-generated key persisted to file and reused |
| `test_config_secret_key_not_default` | Config class never uses old hardcoded default |
| `test_evaluate_user_anomalies_creates_flags` | evaluate_user_anomalies persists AnomalyFlag rows |
| `test_evaluate_user_anomalies_deduplicates` | Second call creates no duplicate flags |
| `test_evaluate_user_anomalies_no_anomaly_no_flags` | No anomalies = no flags created |
| `test_login_triggers_anomaly_evaluation` | Real login triggers automatic anomaly evaluation |
| `test_purge_archives_before_delete` | Purge creates CSV archive before deleting |
| `test_purge_no_expired_returns_zero` | No expired logs = 0 deleted, nothing archived |
| `test_append_only_within_retention_window` | ORM update/delete blocked for recent logs |

Unit tests:  117 passed, 0 failed
API tests:   160 passed, 0 failed
E2E tests:    14 passed, 0 failed
Total:       291 passed, 0 failed
```

### 5) Assumptions

- **F-001**: `scope:dept` uses the user's `CohortMember` rows to infer their department (via major → sub_department → department). This is the existing pattern; no new user-to-department mapping table was added.
- **F-002**: Auto-generated keys are persisted to `data/.secret_key` (configurable via `SECRET_KEY_FILE` env var). If filesystem is read-only (e.g. ephemeral CI), the key exists only in-memory for that session.
- **F-003**: Anomaly evaluation on login is lightweight (single-user queries only). The manual scan endpoint (`POST /admin/anomalies/scan`) is preserved for bulk admin review.
- **F-004**: Archive files are stored in `data/audit_archive/` (configurable via `AUDIT_ARCHIVE_DIR`). The raw SQL delete bypasses ORM listeners intentionally — this is the only authorized deletion path, and only after archiving.

98	## 5. Issues / Suggestions (Severity-Rated)
99	
100	### Blocker / High
101	
102	#### F-001
103	- **Severity**: High
104	- **Title**: Temporary delegation scope hierarchy is inconsistently enforced
105	- **Conclusion**: Fail
106	- **Evidence**
107	  - Delegation input validator excludes some documented/implemented scopes (`subdept`, `self`): `app/routes/permissions.py:15`
108	  - Delegation scope resolver only interprets `scope:cohort:<id>` and `scope:global`: `app/services/rbac_service.py:242`
109	  - `can_access_cohort()` relies on `_scope_permits_cohort()` (UserPermission rows only) + delegation ID set; non-cohort delegation scopes are not equivalently enforced: `app/services/rbac_service.py:401`, `app/services/rbac_service.py:451`
110	- **Impact**
111	  - Scope behavior for temporary delegations can diverge from prompt-required hierarchical model and from documented scope matrix, creating authorization correctness risk.
112	- **Minimum actionable fix**
113	  - Make one canonical scope parser/resolver for both `UserPermission` and `TemporaryDelegation`.
114	  - Extend delegation normalization/validation to all supported scope forms (including `subdept` and any intended `self` semantics).
115	  - Update `can_access_cohort()` to evaluate delegation scopes through the same hierarchy resolver rather than cohort/global special-casing.
116	  - Add API tests for delegated `dept/subdept/school/major/class` access allow/deny boundaries.
117	
118	### Medium / Low
119	
120	#### F-002
121	- **Severity**: Medium
122	- **Title**: Delegation scope test coverage is narrow versus implemented scope matrix
123	- **Conclusion**: Partial Pass
124	- **Evidence**: `tests/api/test_admin_api.py:639`, `tests/unit/test_rbac_service.py:250`
125	- **Impact**: severe scope bugs in non-cohort delegation paths could remain undetected.
126	- **Minimum actionable fix**: add API tests for delegated `scope:dept`, `scope:subdept`, `scope:school`, `scope:major`, `scope:class` positive and negative cases.
127	
128	#### F-003
129	- **Severity**: Low
130	- **Title**: README local dev example uses weak-looking `SECRET_KEY` sample
131	- **Conclusion**: Partial Pass
132	- **Evidence**: `README.md:89`, `app/config.py:29`
133	- **Impact**: onboarding confusion (example value is rejected and replaced by auto-generated key).
134	- **Minimum actionable fix**: replace local example with strong random generation command.

29	def _get_user_school_ids(user) -> set[int]:
30	    """Return school IDs associated with *user* via their CohortMember rows.
31	
32	    Legacy fallback for ``scope:dept`` when majors are not yet linked to
33	    :class:`~app.models.org.SubDepartment` rows.
34	    """
35	    cohort_ids = [cm.cohort_id for cm in CohortMember.query.filter_by(user_id=user.id).all()]
36	    if not cohort_ids:
37	        return set()
38	    rows = (
39	        db.session.query(Major.school_id)
40	        .join(Class, Class.major_id == Major.id)
41	        .join(Cohort, Cohort.class_id == Class.id)
42	        .filter(Cohort.id.in_(cohort_ids))
43	        .distinct()
44	        .all()
45	    )
46	    return {row[0] for row in rows if row[0]}
47

50	    """Department IDs implied by the user's cohort memberships (via major → sub-department)."""
51	    cohort_ids = [cm.cohort_id for cm in CohortMember.query.filter_by(user_id=user.id).all()]
52	    if not cohort_ids:
53	        return set()
54	    rows = (
55	        db.session.query(SubDepartment.department_id)
56	        .join(Major, Major.sub_department_id == SubDepartment.id)
57	        .join(Class, Class.major_id == Major.id)
58	        .join(Cohort, Cohort.class_id == Class.id)
59	        .filter(Cohort.id.in_(cohort_ids), Major.sub_department_id.isnot(None))
60	        .distinct()
61	        .all()
62	    )
63	    return {row[0] for row in rows if row[0]}

91	def resolve_scope(user, scopes: set[str] | None = None) -> set[int]:
92	    """Resolve a set of scope strings into concrete cohort IDs.
93	
94	    Scope strings supported:
95	        scope:global          – all active cohorts
96	        scope:self            – cohorts where user is a member
97	        scope:dept            – cohorts in user's own department(s) + sub-departments
98	        scope:subdept:<id>    – cohorts under a specific sub-department
99	        scope:school:<id>     – cohorts under a school
100	        scope:major:<id>      – cohorts under a major
101	        scope:class:<id>      – cohorts under a class
102	        scope:cohort:<id>     – a single cohort
103	
104	    If *scopes* is ``None`` the user's ``UserPermission`` rows are read.
105	    """
106	    if scopes is None:
107	        scopes = {
108	            up.permission
109	            for up in UserPermission.query.filter_by(user_id=user.id).all()
110	            if up.permission.startswith("scope:")
111	        }
112	
113	    cohort_ids: set[int] = set()
114	
115	    if "scope:global" in scopes:
116	        return {c.id for c in Cohort.query.filter(Cohort.is_active.is_(True)).all()}
117	
118	    if "scope:self" in scopes:
119	        cohort_ids.update(
120	            cm.cohort_id for cm in CohortMember.query.filter_by(user_id=user.id).all()
121	        )
122	
123	    if "scope:dept" in scopes:
124	        user_dept_ids = _get_user_department_ids(user)
125	        for dept_id in user_dept_ids:
126	            cohort_ids.update(_get_dept_and_subdept_cohort_ids(dept_id))
127	        if not user_dept_ids:
128	            # Legacy fallback: use school-level match
129	            dept_school_ids = _get_user_school_ids(user)
130	            if dept_school_ids:
131	                ids = (
132	                    db.session.query(Cohort.id)
133	                    .join(Class, Class.id == Cohort.class_id)
134	                    .join(Major, Major.id == Class.major_id)
135	                    .filter(Major.school_id.in_(dept_school_ids), Cohort.is_active.is_(True))
136	                    .all()
137	                )
138	                cohort_ids.update(row[0] for row in ids)
139	
140	    for s in scopes:
141	        if s.startswith("scope:subdept:"):
142	            try:
143	                subdept_id = int(s.split(":")[-1])
144	                cohort_ids.update(_get_subdept_cohort_ids(subdept_id))
145	            except (ValueError, IndexError):
146	                pass
147	        elif s.startswith("scope:school:"):
148	            try:
149	                school_id = int(s.split(":")[-1])
150	                ids = (
151	                    db.session.query(Cohort.id)
152	                    .join(Class, Class.id == Cohort.class_id)
153	                    .join(Major, Major.id == Class.major_id)
154	                    .filter(Major.school_id == school_id, Cohort.is_active.is_(True))
155	                    .all()
156	                )
157	                cohort_ids.update(row[0] for row in ids)
158	            except (ValueError, IndexError):
159	                pass
160	        elif s.startswith("scope:major:"):
161	            try:
162	                major_id = int(s.split(":")[-1])
163	                ids = (
164	                    db.session.query(Cohort.id)
165	                    .join(Class, Class.id == Cohort.class_id)
166	                    .filter(Class.major_id == major_id, Cohort.is_active.is_(True))
167	                    .all()
168	                )
169	                cohort_ids.update(row[0] for row in ids)
170	            except (ValueError, IndexError):
171	                pass
172	        elif s.startswith("scope:class:"):
173	            try:
174	                class_id = int(s.split(":")[-1])
175	                ids = (
176	                    db.session.query(Cohort.id)
177	                    .filter(Cohort.class_id == class_id, Cohort.is_active.is_(True))
178	                    .all()
179	                )
180	                cohort_ids.update(row[0] for row in ids)
181	            except (ValueError, IndexError):
182	                pass
183	        elif s.startswith("scope:cohort:"):
184	            try:
185	                cohort_ids.add(int(s.split(":")[-1]))
186	            except (ValueError, IndexError):
187	                pass
188	
189	    return cohort_ids

192	def _json_list(raw: str | None) -> list[str]:
193	    if not raw:
194	        return []
195	    try:
196	        parsed = json.loads(raw)
197	    except json.JSONDecodeError:
198	        return []
199	    if isinstance(parsed, list):
200	        return [str(x) for x in parsed]
201	    return []

204	def get_user_permissions(user, effective_role: str | None = None) -> set[str]:
205	    """Return the active permission set for *user*.
206	
207	    When *effective_role* is given (e.g. the current session role after a
208	    role-switch) the base permissions for that role are used instead of the
209	    user's stored primary role.  Explicit grants and active delegations are
210	    always added on top, but only when they are not blocked by the narrower
211	    effective role.  The narrowing rule: if the effective role is ``student``
212	    we cap delegation/explicit extras to only those permissions also present
213	    in the student base set, preventing privilege escalation through grants
214	    that were issued for the primary role.
215	    """
216	    resolved_role = effective_role if effective_role else user.role
217	    base = set(ROLE_PERMISSIONS.get(resolved_role, set()))
218	    now = datetime.now(timezone.utc).replace(tzinfo=None)
219	
220	    explicit = UserPermission.query.filter(UserPermission.user_id == user.id).all()
221	    for row in explicit:
222	        if row.expires_at and row.expires_at < now:
223	            continue
224	        base.add(row.permission)
225	
226	    delegations = TemporaryDelegation.query.filter(
227	        TemporaryDelegation.delegate_id == user.id,
228	        TemporaryDelegation.is_active.is_(True),
229	        TemporaryDelegation.expires_at >= now,
230	    ).all()
231	    for delegation in delegations:
232	        for permission in _json_list(delegation.permissions):
233	            base.add(permission)
234	
235	    return base
236	
237	
238	def has_permission(user, permission: str, effective_role: str | None = None) -> bool:
239	    return permission in get_user_permissions(user, effective_role=effective_role)

242	def get_delegation_cohort_ids(user) -> set[int] | None:
243	    """
244	    Returns the set of cohort IDs accessible via active delegations,
245	    or None if any active delegation has global scope (no restriction).
246	    Returns an empty set if the user has no active delegations.
247	    """
248	    now = datetime.now(timezone.utc).replace(tzinfo=None)
249	    delegations = TemporaryDelegation.query.filter(
250	        TemporaryDelegation.delegate_id == user.id,
251	        TemporaryDelegation.is_active.is_(True),
252	        TemporaryDelegation.expires_at >= now,
253	    ).all()
254	
255	    if not delegations:
256	        return set()
257	
258	    cohort_ids: set[int] = set()
259	    for d in delegations:
260	        scope = (d.scope or "").strip()
261	        if not scope or scope == "scope:global":
262	            return None
263	        if scope.startswith("scope:cohort:"):
264	            try:
265	                cohort_ids.add(int(scope.split(":")[-1]))
266	            except (ValueError, IndexError):
267	                pass
268	    return cohort_ids

271	def get_accessible_cohorts(user, effective_role: str | None = None) -> list[Cohort]:
272	    role = effective_role or user.role
273	    if role == "dept_admin":
274	        return Cohort.query.filter(Cohort.is_active.is_(True)).all()
275	
276	    scope_cohort_ids: set[int] = set()
277	    scopes = {
278	        up.permission
279	        for up in UserPermission.query.filter_by(user_id=user.id).all()
280	        if up.permission.startswith("scope:")
281	    }
282	    if "scope:global" in scopes:
283	        return Cohort.query.filter(Cohort.is_active.is_(True)).all()
284	
285	    # scope:dept — same department (and all its sub-departments) as the user's cohort memberships.
286	    if "scope:dept" in scopes:
287	        user_dept_ids = _get_user_department_ids(user)
288	        if user_dept_ids:
289	            ids = (
290	                db.session.query(Cohort.id)
291	                .join(Class, Class.id == Cohort.class_id)
292	                .join(Major, Major.id == Class.major_id)
293	                .join(SubDepartment, SubDepartment.id == Major.sub_department_id)
294	                .filter(SubDepartment.department_id.in_(user_dept_ids), Cohort.is_active.is_(True))
295	                .all()
296	            )
297	            scope_cohort_ids.update(row[0] for row in ids)
298	        else:
299	            dept_school_ids = _get_user_school_ids(user)
300	            if dept_school_ids:
301	                ids = (
302	                    db.session.query(Cohort.id)
303	                    .join(Class, Class.id == Cohort.class_id)
304	                    .join(Major, Major.id == Class.major_id)
305	                    .filter(Major.school_id.in_(dept_school_ids), Cohort.is_active.is_(True))
306	                    .all()
307	                )
308	                scope_cohort_ids.update(row[0] for row in ids)
309	
310	    for s in scopes:
311	        if s.startswith("scope:school:"):
312	            school_id = int(s.split(":")[-1])
313	            ids = (
314	                db.session.query(Cohort.id)
315	                .join(Class, Class.id == Cohort.class_id)
316	                .join(Major, Major.id == Class.major_id)
317	                .filter(Major.school_id == school_id, Cohort.is_active.is_(True))
318	                .all()
319	            )
320	            scope_cohort_ids.update(row[0] for row in ids)
321	
322	    for s in scopes:
323	        if s.startswith("scope:subdept:"):
324	            try:
325	                subdept_id = int(s.split(":")[-1])
326	                scope_cohort_ids.update(_get_subdept_cohort_ids(subdept_id))
327	            except (ValueError, IndexError):
328	                pass
329	
330	    for s in scopes:
331	        if s.startswith("scope:major:"):
332	            major_id = int(s.split(":")[-1])
333	            ids = (
334	                db.session.query(Cohort.id)
335	                .join(Class, Class.id == Cohort.class_id)
336	                .filter(Class.major_id == major_id, Cohort.is_active.is_(True))
337	                .all()
338	            )
339	            scope_cohort_ids.update(row[0] for row in ids)
340	
341	    for s in scopes:
342	        if s.startswith("scope:class:"):
343	            class_id = int(s.split(":")[-1])
344	            ids = (
345	                db.session.query(Cohort.id)
346	                .filter(Cohort.class_id == class_id, Cohort.is_active.is_(True))
347	                .all()
348	            )
349	            scope_cohort_ids.update(row[0] for row in ids)
350	
351	    for s in scopes:
352	        if s.startswith("scope:cohort:"):
353	            scope_cohort_ids.add(int(s.split(":")[-1]))
354	
355	    member_ids = {
356	        a.cohort_id for a in CohortMember.query.filter_by(user_id=user.id).all()
357	    }
358	    cohort_ids = scope_cohort_ids | member_ids
359	
360	    delegation_ids = get_delegation_cohort_ids(user)
361	    if delegation_ids is not None and len(delegation_ids) > 0:
362	        directly_accessible = {cm.cohort_id for cm in CohortMember.query.filter_by(user_id=user.id).all()}
363	        for cid in list(cohort_ids):
364	            if cid not in directly_accessible and cid not in delegation_ids:
365	                cohort_ids.discard(cid)
366	
367	    if not cohort_ids:
368	        return []
369	    return Cohort.query.filter(Cohort.id.in_(cohort_ids), Cohort.is_active.is_(True)).all()

372	def get_accessible_schools(user, effective_role: str | None = None) -> list[School]:
373	    role = effective_role or user.role
374	    if role == "dept_admin":
375	        explicit = [
376	            up.permission.split(":")[-1]
377	            for up in UserPermission.query.filter(UserPermission.user_id == user.id).all()
378	            if up.permission.startswith("scope:school:")
379	        ]

382	            return School.query.filter(School.is_active.is_(True), School.id.in_(school_ids)).all()
383	        return School.query.filter(School.is_active.is_(True)).all()
384

401	def _scope_permits_cohort(user, cohort_id: int) -> bool:
402	    """
403	    Returns True if any explicit scope permission in UserPermission grants
404	    access to the given cohort via the org hierarchy:
405	      scope:global
406	      scope:school:<id>  -> all cohorts in that school
407	      scope:major:<id>   -> all cohorts in that major
408	      scope:class:<id>   -> all cohorts in that class
409	      scope:cohort:<id>  -> that specific cohort
410	    """
411	    cohort = db.session.get(Cohort, cohort_id)
412	    if not cohort:
413	        return False
414	    klass = db.session.get(Class, cohort.class_id) if cohort.class_id else None
415	    major = db.session.get(Major, klass.major_id) if klass and klass.major_id else None
416	    school = db.session.get(School, major.school_id) if major and major.school_id else None
417	    scopes_granted = {
418	        up.permission
419	        for up in UserPermission.query.filter_by(user_id=user.id).all()
420	        if up.permission.startswith("scope:")
421	    }
422	    if "scope:global" in scopes_granted:
423	        return True
424	    # scope:dept — same department tree as cohort memberships, or legacy school match.
425	    if "scope:dept" in scopes_granted:
426	        user_dept_ids = _get_user_department_ids(user)
427	        if major and major.sub_department_id:
428	            sd = db.session.get(SubDepartment, major.sub_department_id)
429	            if sd and sd.department_id in user_dept_ids:
430	                return True
431	        user_school_ids = _get_user_school_ids(user)
432	        if school and school.id in user_school_ids:
433	            return True
434	    if school and f"scope:school:{school.id}" in scopes_granted:
435	        return True
436	    if major and major.sub_department_id and f"scope:subdept:{major.sub_department_id}" in scopes_granted:
437	        return True
438	    if major and f"scope:major:{major.id}" in scopes_granted:
439	        return True
440	    if klass and f"scope:class:{klass.id}" in scopes_granted:
441	        return True
442	    if f"scope:cohort:{cohort_id}" in scopes_granted:
443	        return True
444	    # scope:self — user is a member of this cohort
445	    if "scope:self" in scopes_granted:
446	        if CohortMember.query.filter_by(user_id=user.id, cohort_id=cohort_id).first():
447	            return True
448	    return False
449	
450	
451	def can_access_cohort(user, cohort_id: int, effective_role: str | None = None) -> bool:
452	    role = effective_role or user.role
453	    if role == "dept_admin":
454	        return Cohort.query.filter_by(id=cohort_id).first() is not None
455	    if _scope_permits_cohort(user, cohort_id):
456	        return True
457	    if (
458	        CohortMember.query.filter(
459	            CohortMember.user_id == user.id,
460	            CohortMember.cohort_id == cohort_id,
461	        ).first()
462	        is not None
463	    ):
464	        return True
465	
466	    delegation_ids = get_delegation_cohort_ids(user)
467	    if delegation_ids is None:
468	        return True
469	    return cohort_id in delegation_ids
470

472	def can_access_student(user, student_id: int, effective_role: str | None = None) -> bool:
473	    role = effective_role or user.role
474	    if role == "dept_admin":
475	        return True
476	    if role == "student":
477	        return user.id == student_id
478	
479	    my_cohort_ids = [r.cohort_id for r in CohortMember.query.filter(CohortMember.user_id == user.id).all()]
480	    if not my_cohort_ids:
481	        return False
482	    shared = (
483	        CohortMember.query.filter(
484	            CohortMember.user_id == student_id,
485	            CohortMember.role_in_cohort == "student",
486	            CohortMember.cohort_id.in_(my_cohort_ids),
487	        ).first()

493	def expire_delegations() -> int:
494	    now = datetime.now(timezone.utc).replace(tzinfo=None)

497	        TemporaryDelegation.expires_at < now,

29	def _get_user_school_ids(user) -> set[int]:
30	    """Return school IDs associated with *user* via their CohortMember rows.
31	
32	    Legacy fallback for ``scope:dept`` when majors are not yet linked to
33	    :class:`~app.models.org.SubDepartment` rows.
34	    """
35	    cohort_ids = [cm.cohort_id for cm in CohortMember.query.filter_by(user_id=user.id).all()]
36	    if not cohort_ids:
37	        return set()
38	    rows = (
39	        db.session.query(Major.school_id)

41	        .join(Cohort, Cohort.class_id == Class.id)
42	        .filter(Cohort.id.in_(cohort_ids))
43	        .distinct()
44	        .all()
45	    )
46	    return {row[0] for row in rows if row[0]}

91	def resolve_scope(user, scopes: set[str] | None = None) -> set[int]:
92	    """Resolve a set of scope strings into concrete cohort IDs.
93	
94	    Scope strings supported:
95	        scope:global          – all active cohorts
96	        scope:self            – cohorts where user is a member
97	        scope:dept            – cohorts in user's own department(s) + sub-departments
98	        scope:subdept:<id>    – cohorts under a specific sub-department
99	        scope:school:<id>     – cohorts under a school
100	        scope:major:<id>      – cohorts under a major
101	        scope:class:<id>      – cohorts under a class
102	        scope:cohort:<id>     – a single cohort
103	
104	    If *scopes* is ``None`` the user's ``UserPermission`` rows are read.
105	    """
106	    if scopes is None:
107	        scopes = {
108	            up.permission
109	            for up in UserPermission.query.filter_by(user_id=user.id).all()
110	            if up.permission.startswith("scope:")
111	        }
112	
113	    cohort_ids: set[int] = set()
114	
115	    if "scope:global" in scopes:
116	        return {c.id for c in Cohort.query.filter(Cohort.is_active.is_(True)).all()}
117	
118	    if "scope:self" in scopes:
119	        cohort_ids.update(
120	            cm.cohort_id for cm in CohortMember.query.filter_by(user_id=user.id).all()
121	        )
122	
123	    if "scope:dept" in scopes:
124	        user_dept_ids = _get_user_department_ids(user)
125	        for dept_id in user_dept_ids:
126	            cohort_ids.update(_get_dept_and_subdept_cohort_ids(dept_id))
127	        if not user_dept_ids:
128	            # Legacy fallback: use school-level match
129	            dept_school_ids = _get_user_school_ids(user)
130	            if dept_school_ids:
131	                ids = (
132	                    db.session.query(Cohort.id)
133	                    .join(Class, Class.id == Cohort.class_id)
134	                    .join(Major, Major.id == Class.major_id)
135	                    .filter(Major.school_id.in_(dept_school_ids), Cohort.is_active.is_(True))
136	                    .all()
137	                )
138	                cohort_ids.update(row[0] for row in ids)
139	
140	    for s in scopes:
141	        if s.startswith("scope:subdept:"):
142	            try:
143	                subdept_id = int(s.split(":")[-1])
144	                cohort_ids.update(_get_subdept_cohort_ids(subdept_id))
145	            except (ValueError, IndexError):
146	                pass
147	        elif s.startswith("scope:school:"):
148	            try:
149	                school_id = int(s.split(":")[-1])
150	                ids = (
151	                    db.session.query(Cohort.id)
152	                    .join(Class, Class.id == Cohort.class_id)
153	                    .join(Major, Major.id == Class.major_id)
154	                    .filter(Major.school_id == school_id, Cohort.is_active.is_(True))
155	                    .all()
156	                )
157	                cohort_ids.update(row[0] for row in ids)
158	            except (ValueError, IndexError):
159	                pass
160	        elif s.startswith("scope:major:"):
161	            try:
162	                major_id = int(s.split(":")[-1])
163	                ids = (
164	                    db.session.query(Cohort.id)
165	                    .join(Class, Class.id == Cohort.class_id)
166	                    .filter(Class.major_id == major_id, Cohort.is_active.is_(True))
167	                    .all()
168	                )
169	                cohort_ids.update(row[0] for row in ids)
170	            except (ValueError, IndexError):
171	                pass
172	        elif s.startswith("scope:class:"):
173	            try:
174	                class_id = int(s.split(":")[-1])
175	                ids = (
176	                    db.session.query(Cohort.id)
177	                    .filter(Cohort.class_id == class_id, Cohort.is_active.is_(True))
178	                    .all()
179	                )
180	                cohort_ids.update(row[0] for row in ids)
181	            except (ValueError, IndexError):
182	                pass
183	        elif s.startswith("scope:cohort:"):
184	            try:
185	                cohort_ids.add(int(s.split(":")[-1]))
186	            except (ValueError, IndexError):
187	                pass
188	
189	    return cohort_ids

192	def _json_list(raw: str | None) -> list[str]:
193	    if not raw:
194	        return []
195	    try:
196	        parsed = json.loads(raw)
197	    except json.JSONDecodeError:
198	        return []
199	    if isinstance(parsed, list):
200	        return [str(x) for x in parsed]
201	    return []

204	def get_user_permissions(user, effective_role: str | None = None) -> set[str]:
205	    """Return the active permission set for *user*.
206	
207	    When *effective_role* is given (e.g. the current session role after a
208	    role-switch) the base permissions for that role are used instead of the
209	    user's stored primary role.  Explicit grants and active delegations are
210	    always added on top, but only when they are not blocked by the narrower
211	    effective role.  The narrowing rule: if the effective role is ``student``
212	    we cap delegation/explicit extras to only those permissions also present
213	    in the student base set, preventing privilege escalation through grants
214	    that were issued for the primary role.
215	    """
216	    resolved_role = effective_role if effective_role else user.role
217	    base = set(ROLE_PERMISSIONS.get(resolved_role, set()))
218	    now = datetime.now(timezone.utc).replace(tzinfo=None)
219	
220	    explicit = UserPermission.query.filter(UserPermission.user_id == user.id).all()
221	    for row in explicit:
222	        if row.expires_at and row.expires_at < now:
223	            continue
224	        base.add(row.permission)
225	
226	    delegations = TemporaryDelegation.query.filter(
227	        TemporaryDelegation.delegate_id == user.id,
228	        TemporaryDelegation.is_active.is_(True),
229	        TemporaryDelegation.expires_at >= now,
230	    ).all()
231	    for delegation in delegations:
232	        for permission in _json_list(delegation.permissions):
233	            base.add(permission)
234	
235	    return base
236	
237	
238	def has_permission(user, permission: str, effective_role: str | None = None) -> bool:
239	    return permission in get_user_permissions(user, effective_role=effective_role)

242	def get_delegation_cohort_ids(user) -> set[int] | None:
243	    """
244	    Returns the set of cohort IDs accessible via active delegations,
245	    or None if any active delegation has global scope (no restriction).
246	    Returns an empty set if the user has no active delegations.
247	    """
248	    now = datetime.now(timezone.utc).replace(tzinfo=None)
249	    delegations = TemporaryDelegation.query.filter(
250	        TemporaryDelegation.delegate_id == user.id,
251	        TemporaryDelegation.is_active.is_(True),
252	        TemporaryDelegation.expires_at >= now,
253	    ).all()
254	
255	    if not delegations:
256	        return set()
257	
258	    cohort_ids: set[int] = set()
259	    for d in delegations:
260	        scope = (d.scope or "").strip()
261	        if not scope or scope == "scope:global":
262	            return None
263	        if scope.startswith("scope:cohort:"):
264	            try:
265	                cohort_ids.add(int(scope.split(":")[-1]))
266	            except (ValueError, IndexError):
267	                pass
268	    return cohort_ids

305	                    .filter(Major.school_id.in_(dept_school_ids), Cohort.is_active.is_(True))
306	                    .all()
307	                )

309	
310	    for s in scopes:

317	                .filter(Major.school_id == school_id, Cohort.is_active.is_(True))
318	                .all()
319	            )
320	            scope_cohort_ids.update(row[0] for row in ids)
321	
322	    for s in scopes:
323	        if s.startswith("scope:subdept:"):
324	            try:
325	                subdept_id = int(s.split(":")[-1])
326	                scope_cohort_ids.update(_get_subdept_cohort_ids(subdept_id))

329	
330	    for s in scopes:
331	        if s.startswith("scope:major:"):

333	            ids = (

336	                .filter(Class.major_id == major_id, Cohort.is_active.is_(True))
337	                .all()

341	    for s in scopes:

363	        for cid in list(cohort_ids):

372	def get_accessible_schools(user, effective_role: str | None = None) -> list[School]:

383	        return School.query.filter(School.is_active.is_(True)).all()
