401	def _scope_permits_cohort(user, cohort_id: int) -> bool:
402	    """
403	    Returns True if any explicit scope permission in UserPermission grants
404	    access to the given cohort via the org hierarchy:
405	      scope:global
406	      scope:school:<id>  -> all cohorts in that school
407	      scope:major:<id>   -> all cohorts in that major
408	      scope:class:<id>   -> all cohorts in that class
409	      scope:cohort:<id>  -> that specific cohort
410	    """
411	    cohort = db.session.get(Cohort, cohort_id)
412	    if not cohort:
413	        return False
414	    klass = db.session.get(Class, cohort.class_id) if cohort.class_id else None
415	    major = db.session.get(Major, klass.major_id) if klass and klass.major_id else None
416	    school = db.session.get(School, major.school_id) if major and major.school_id else None
417	    scopes_granted = {
418	        up.permission
419	        for up in UserPermission.query.filter_by(user_id=user.id).all()
420	        if up.permission.startswith("scope:")
421	    }
422	    if "scope:global" in scopes_granted:
423	        return True
424	    # scope:dept — same department tree as cohort memberships, or legacy school match.
425	    if "scope:dept" in scopes_granted:
426	        user_dept_ids = _get_user_department_ids(user)
427	        if major and major.sub_department_id:
428	            sd = db.session.get(SubDepartment, major.sub_department_id)
429	            if sd and sd.department_id in user_dept_ids:
430	                return True
431	        user_school_ids = _get_user_school_ids(user)
432	        if school and school.id in user_school_ids:
433	            return True
434	    if school and f"scope:school:{school.id}" in scopes_granted:
435	        return True
436	    if major and major.sub_department_id and f"scope:subdept:{major.sub_department_id}" in scopes_granted:
437	        return True
438	    if major and f"scope:major:{major.id}" in scopes_granted:
439	        return True
440	    if klass and f"scope:class:{klass.id}" in scopes_granted:
441	        return True
442	    if f"scope:cohort:{cohort_id}" in scopes_granted:
443	        return True
444	    # scope:self — user is a member of this cohort
445	    if "scope:self" in scopes_granted:
446	        if CohortMember.query.filter_by(user_id=user.id, cohort_id=cohort_id).first():
447	            return True
448	    return False
449

451	def can_access_cohort(user, cohort_id: int, effective_role: str | None = None) -> bool:
452	    role = effective_role or user.role
453	    if role == "dept_admin":
454	        return Cohort.query.filter_by(id=cohort_id).first() is not None
455	    if _scope_permits_cohort(user, cohort_id):
456	        return True
457	    if (
458	        CohortMember.query.filter(
459	            CohortMember.user_id == user.id,
460	            CohortMember.cohort_id == cohort_id,
461	        ).first()
462	        is not None
463	    ):
464	        return True
465	
466	    delegation_ids = get_delegation_cohort_ids(user)
467	    if delegation_ids is None:
468	        return True
469	    return cohort_id in delegation_ids
470

472	def can_access_student(user, student_id: int, effective_role: str | None = None) -> bool:
473	    role = effective_role or user.role
474	    if role == "dept_admin":
475	        return True
476	    if role == "student":
477	        return user.id == student_id
478	
479	    my_cohort_ids = [r.cohort_id for r in CohortMember.query.filter(CohortMember.user_id == user.id).all()]
480	    if not my_cohort_ids:
481	        return False
482	    shared = (
483	        CohortMember.query.filter(
484	            CohortMember.user_id == student_id,
485	            CohortMember.role_in_cohort == "student",
486	            CohortMember.cohort_id.in_(my_cohort_ids),
487	        ).first()
488	        is not None
489	    )
490	    return shared
491	
492	
493	def expire_delegations() -> int:
494	    now = datetime.now(timezone.utc).replace(tzinfo=None)
495	    rows = TemporaryDelegation.query.filter(
496	        TemporaryDelegation.is_active.is_(True),
497	        TemporaryDelegation.expires_at < now,
498	    ).all()
499	    for row in rows:
500	        row.is_active = False
501	        db.session.add(row)
502	    db.session.commit()
503	    return len(rows)
504	
505	
506	def get_available_roles(user) -> list[str]:
507	    """Returns the user's primary role plus any extra roles granted via UserPermission."""
508	    roles = [user.role]
509	    extra = [
510	        up.permission[len("role:") :]
511	        for up in UserPermission.query.filter_by(user_id=user.id).all()
512	        if up.permission.startswith("role:")
513	    ]
514	    for role_name in extra:
515	        if role_name not in roles:
516	            roles.append(role_name)
517	    return roles
518	
519	
520	def get_nav_for_role(user, active_role: str | None = None):
521	    if not user:
522	        return []
523	
524	    role = active_role or user.role
525	
526	    nav = {
527	        "dept_admin": [
528	            ("Dashboard", "/dashboard"),
529	            ("Admin", "/admin/dashboard"),
530	            ("Org Structure", "/admin/org/schools"),
531	            ("Question Bank", "/admin/questions"),
532	            ("Papers", "/admin/papers"),
533	            ("Assignments", "/admin/assignments"),
534	            ("Users", "/admin/users"),
535	            ("Reports", "/reports"),
536	            ("Permissions", "/admin/permissions/templates"),
537	            ("Audit Logs", "/admin/audit-logs"),
538	        ],
539	        "faculty_advisor": [
540	            ("Dashboard", "/dashboard"),
541	            ("My Cohorts", "/cohorts"),
542	            ("Grading", "/grading"),
543	            ("Assignment Grading", "/assignments/grading"),
544	            ("Reports", "/reports"),
545	        ],
546	        "corporate_mentor": [
547	            ("Dashboard", "/dashboard"),
548	            ("My Cohorts", "/cohorts"),
549	            ("Grading", "/grading"),
550	            ("Assignment Grading", "/assignments/grading"),
551	            ("Reports", "/reports"),
552	        ],

554	            ("Dashboard", "/dashboard"),
555	            ("My Assessments", "/quiz"),
556	            ("Assignments", "/assignments"),
557	        ],
558	    }
559	    items = nav.get(role, [])
560	    items = list(items) + [("Switch Role", "/switch-role")]
561	    return [{"label": label, "href": href} for label, href in items]

639	def test_delegation_grants_access_to_delegated_cohort(client, app, admin_user, seeded_assessment):
640	    """End-to-end: admin creates delegation for faculty_advisor via route; delegate can
641	    access the delegated cohort detail page but NOT a cohort outside the scope."""
642	    from tests.conftest import create_user
643	    from app.services.auth_service import hash_password
644	
645	    cohort_id = seeded_assessment["cohort_id"]
646	    cohort2_id = seeded_assessment["cohort2_id"]
647	
648	    with app.app_context():
649	        advisor = create_user("effect_advisor", "faculty_advisor", "Advisor@Practicum1")
650	        advisor_id = advisor.id
651	        admin_id = User.query.filter_by(username="admin").first().id
652	
653	    # Step 1: admin creates delegation with canonical scope
654	    client.post("/login", data={"username": "admin", "password": "Admin@Practicum1"})
655	    _reauth_session(client, "create_delegation")
656	    res = client.post(
657	        "/admin/permissions/delegations",
658	        data={
659	            "delegator_id": str(admin_id),
660	            "delegate_id": str(advisor_id),
661	            "scope": f"scope:cohort:{cohort_id}",
662	            "permissions": "cohort:view",
663	            "expires_in_days": "7",
664	        },
665	        headers={"HX-Request": "true"},
666	    )
667	    assert res.status_code == 200
668	
669	    # Step 2: login as the advisor (no cohort membership)
670	    client.get("/logout", follow_redirects=True)
671	    client.post("/login", data={"username": "effect_advisor", "password": "Advisor@Practicum1"})
672	
673	    # Step 3: advisor CAN access the delegated cohort
674	    res_allowed = client.get(f"/cohorts/{cohort_id}", follow_redirects=False)
675	    assert res_allowed.status_code == 200, (
676	        f"Advisor with delegated scope:cohort:{cohort_id} must be able to access /cohorts/{cohort_id}"
677	    )
678	
679	    # Step 4: advisor CANNOT access a different cohort outside the delegation scope
680	    res_denied = client.get(f"/cohorts/{cohort2_id}", follow_redirects=False)
681	    assert res_denied.status_code == 403, (
682	        f"Advisor must NOT access cohort {cohort2_id} which is outside the delegated scope"
683	    )

    return cohort_ids", "new_string": "def get_delegation_cohort_ids(user) -> set[int] | None:
    """Return the set of cohort IDs accessible via active delegations.

    All delegation scopes are resolved through the canonical
    :func:`resolve_scope` resolver so that ``scope:dept``,
    ``scope:subdept:<id>``, ``scope:school:<id>``, ``scope:major:<id>``,
    ``scope:class:<id>``, ``scope:self``, ``scope:cohort:<id>``, and
    ``scope:global`` are all evaluated identically to ``UserPermission``
    scopes.

    Returns ``None`` if any active delegation has global scope (unrestricted).
    Returns an empty ``set()`` if the user has no active delegations.
    """
    now = datetime.now(timezone.utc).replace(tzinfo=None)
    delegations = TemporaryDelegation.query.filter(
        TemporaryDelegation.delegate_id == user.id,
        TemporaryDelegation.is_active.is_(True),
        TemporaryDelegation.expires_at >= now,
    ).all()

    if not delegations:
        return set()

    # Collect all delegation scope strings, then resolve through the
    # canonical hierarchy resolver.
    scopes: set[str] = set()
    for d in delegations:
        scope = (d.scope or "").strip()
        if not scope or scope == "scope:global":
            return None  # unrestricted
        scopes.add(scope)

    return resolve_scope(user, scopes)"}

    for d in delegations:

        if not scope or scope == "scope:global":

        scopes.add(scope)

def _make_delegated_user(app, username, seeded_assessment, scope, member_cohort_id=None):
    """Create a user with a single active delegation of the given scope."""
    with app.app_context():
        user = User(username=username, password_hash=hash_password("Test1234!@Ab"), role="faculty_advisor")
        db.session.add(user)
        db.session.flush()
        if member_cohort_id:
            db.session.add(CohortMember(cohort_id=member_cohort_id, user_id=user.id, role_in_cohort="faculty_advisor"))
        delegation = TemporaryDelegation(
            delegator_id=seeded_assessment["admin_id"],
            delegate_id=user.id,
            scope=scope,
            permissions='["cohort:view","cohort:grade"]',
            expires_at=datetime.now(timezone.utc).replace(tzinfo=None) + timedelta(days=1),
            is_active=True,
        )
        db.session.add(delegation)
        db.session.commit()
        return user


def _get_org_ids(app, cohort_id):

    """Create a user with a single active delegation of the given scope."""
    with app.app_context():
        user = User(username=username, password_hash=hash_password("Test1234!@Ab"), role="faculty_advisor")
        db.session.add(user)

def _make_delegated_user(app, username, seeded_assessment, scope, member_cohort_id=None):
    """Create a user with a single active delegation of the given scope."""
    with app.app_context():
        user = User(username=username, password_hash=hash_password("Test1234!@Ab"), role="faculty_advisor")
        db.session.add(user)
        db.session.flush()
        if member_cohort_id:

            delegator_id=seeded_assessment["admin_id"],
            delegate_id=user.id,
            scope=scope,
            permissions='["cohort:view","cohort:grade"]',
            expires_at=datetime.now(timezone.utc).replace(tzinfo=None) + timedelta(days=1),
            is_active=True,
        )
        db.session.add(delegation)
        db.session.commit()
        return user

    """Return (school_id, dept_id, subdept_id, major_id, class_id) for a cohort."""
    with app.app_context():
        cohort = db.session.get(Cohort, cohort_id)
        klass = db.session.get(Class, cohort.class_id)
        major = db.session.get(Major, klass.major_id)
        subdept = db.session.get(SubDepartment, major.sub_department_id)
        return {
            "school_id": major.school_id,
            "dept_id": subdept.department_id,
            "subdept_id": subdept.id,
            "major_id": major.id,
            "class_id": klass.id,
        }

    """Create a user with a single active delegation of the given scope.

    Returns the user ID (not the ORM object) to avoid DetachedInstanceError.
    Callers must re-query the User inside their own ``app.app_context()``.
    """
    with app.app_context():
        user = User(username=username, password_hash=hash_password("Test1234!@Ab"), role="faculty_advisor")
        db.session.add(user)
        db.session.flush()
        if member_cohort_id:
            db.session.add(CohortMember(cohort_id=member_cohort_id, user_id=user.id, role_in_cohort="faculty_advisor"))
        delegation = TemporaryDelegation(
            delegator_id=seeded_assessment["admin_id"],
            delegate_id=user.id,
            scope=scope,
            permissions='["cohort:view","cohort:grade"]',
            expires_at=datetime.now(timezone.utc).replace(tzinfo=None) + timedelta(days=1),
            is_active=True,
        )
        db.session.add(delegation)
        db.session.commit()
        return user.id"}

----------------------------------------------------------------
OPEN FINDINGS TO FIX
----------------------------------------------------------------

F-001 (High) — Temporary delegation scope hierarchy inconsistently enforced

Current pblem to eliminate:
- Delegation scope input validation/normalization path does not fully align with documented scope matrix.
- Delegation resolution path is not using the same canonical hierarchy logic as direct user permissions for all scope types.
- This can cause delegated access behavior to diverge from required hierarchy semantics.

Required implementation:
A) Canonical scope model for both UserPermission and TemporaryDelegation
- In `app/services/rbac_service.py`, introduce/reuse one canonical resolver that maps scope strings to cohort IDs, and use it for:
  - direct permissions
  - temporary delegation scopes
- Supported scopes must be consistently handled:
  - `scope:global`
  - `scope:self`
  - `scope:dept`
  - `scope:subdept:<id>`
  - `scope:school:<id>`
  - `scope:major:<id>`
  - `scope:class:<id>`
  - `scope:cohort:<id>`

B) Delegation validation/normalization parity
- In `app/routes/permissions.py`, ensure delegation scope validator/normalizer accepts all intended canonical/shorthand forms above.
- Keep strict rejection for malformed scopes.
- Keep error messaging clear and deterministic.

C) Authorization path consistency
- In `app/services/rbac_service.py`, ensure `can_access_cohort()` (and any delegation-related helper path) evaluates delegated scopes via the same hierarchy resolver, not cohort/global-only special casing.
- Preserve deny-by-default behavior.

def get_delegation_cohort_ids(user) -> set[int] | None:
    now = datetime.now(timezone.utc).replace(tzinfo=None)
    delegations = TemporaryDelegation.query.filter(
        TemporaryDelegation.delegate_id == user.id,
        TemporaryDelegation.is_active.is_(True),
        TemporaryDelegation.expires_at >= now,
    ).all()

    if not delegations:
        return set()

    scopes: set[str] = set()
    for d in delegations:
        scope = (d.scope or "").strip()
        if not scope or scope == "scope:global":
            return None  # unrestricted
        scopes.add(scope)

    return resolve_scope(user, scopes)
```

- **`get_delegation_cohort_ids(user)`** — previously returned only `None` (global) or `{cohort_id, ...}` (cohort-only); now must return all cohort IDs resolved from any delegation scope via `resolve_scope()`

            try:
                cohort_ids.add(int(scope.split(":")[-1]))
            except (ValueError, IndexError):
                pass
    return cohort_ids
```
All other scope types (`scope:dept`, `scope:school:<id>`, `scope:major:<id>`, `scope:class:<id>`, `scope:subdept:<id>`, `scope:self`) were silently ignored → authorization divergence.

**After** — uses canonical `resolve_scope()`:
```python
def get_delegation_cohort_ids(user) -> set[int] | None:
    """Return the set of cohort IDs accessible via active delegations.

    All delegation scopes are resolved through the canonical
    resolve_scope() resolver so that scope:dept, scope:subdept:<id>,
    scope:school:<id>, scope:major:<id>, scope:class:<id>, scope:self,
    scope:cohort:<id>, and scope:global are all evaluated identically
    to UserPermission scopes.

    Returns None if any active delegation has global scope (unrestricted).
    Returns empty set() if the user has no active delegations.
    """
    now = datetime.now(timezone.utc).replace(tzinfo=None)
    delegations = TemporaryDelegation.query.filter(
        TemporaryDelegation.delegate_id == user.id,
        TemporaryDelegation.is_active.is_(True),
        TemporaryDelegation.expires_at >= now,
    ).all()

    if not delegations:
        return set()

    scopes: set[str] = set()
    for d in delegations:
        scope = (d.scope or "").strip()
        if not scope or scope == "scope:global":
            return None  # unrestricted
        scopes.add(scope)

    return resolve_scope(user, scopes)
```
